I hit tab in the pkg mode of the REPL I thought that I would get a list of all available commands. 
This list includes `package` but when I try to run it  I get this error:

```julia
(@v1.10) pkg> package
ERROR: `package` is not a recognized command. Type ? for help with available commands
```
and if I try to get the help for `package` I get the expanded help for all available commands.

I find this confusing and would have expected that this entry is not in the autocompletion list.

has been discussed [on Discourse](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/export-markdown-parse/123638).

I've split the PR into 3 commits:

- one for making `parse` public and documenting the public methods,
- one for renaming the non-public methods to `_parse`,

itle":"WIP: App support in Pkg","user":{"login":"KristofferC","id":1282691,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyODI2OTE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1282691?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC","html_url":"https://github.com/KristofferC","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"This is quite heavily WIP towards having "app" support in Pkg. An app is a program that you just write its name in the terminal and it starts up, without explicitly having to invoke Julia, load the package, and call a function. Every app has an isolated environment.

More details of the design can be found in this hackmd: https://hackmd.io/r0sgJar5SpGNomVB8wRP_Q

This PR requires https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52103

Here is some example usage:

```
(Pkg) pkg> app st

shell> rot13
zsh:1: command not found: rot13

(Pkg) pkg> app add https://github.com/KristofferC/Rot13.jl
    Updating git-repo `https://github.com/KristofferC/Rot13.jl`
  Activating project at `~/.julia/environments/apps/Rot13`
  No Changes to `~/.julia/environments/apps/Rot13/Project.toml`
  No Changes to `~/.julia/environments/apps/Rot13/Manifest.toml`

(@Rot13) pkg> app st
[43ef800a] Rot13 v0.1.0 `https://github.com/KristofferC/Rot13.jl#master`:  rot13 /home/kc/julia/usr/bin/julia 

shell> rot13 Rotate this please
Ebgngr
guvf
cyrnfr

(@Rot13) pkg> app rm rot13
[ Info: Deleted app rot13

(@Rot13) pkg> app st
```

cc @MasonProtter, @roger-luo

Project.toml","user":{"login":"fonsp","id":6933510,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY5MzM1MTA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6933510?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp","html_url":"https://github.com/fonsp","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fonsp/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2021-06-22T13:00:15Z","updated_at":"2024-11-12T11:59:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"When launching `julia` with argument `--project`, then it will _"search through parent directories until a Project.toml or JuliaProject.toml file is found."_ ([docs](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/manual/command-line-options/)). This is really useful for projects with scripts and notebooks in nested folders. (e.g. `<project-root>/notebooks/analysis/hello.jl`)

It would be great if this automatic Project search was available as a **function** in Pkg, to be called at the top of a script/notebook. This would be useful in environments where setting the `project` flag is impractical (Pluto.jl, Jupyter, VS Code). It also helps to simplify the reproducibility steps: you can just run the script.

Currently, you need to manually count towards the root directory of your project:

```julia
import Pkg
Pkg.activate(joinpath(@__DIR__, "..", ".."))

# but I wish I could do:
Pkg.activate(; search_parents=true)
```

I would be happy to make this PR myself if there is using a function with multiple keyword arguments, like the one below, a type mismatch in `save_to` leads to an error that is difficult to understand and troubleshoot for regular users.

Suppose we have the following code:

```julia
function experiment(; 
    parameter1::Float64 = 1.0, 
    parameter2::Float64 = 2.0,
    parameter3::Bool = true,
    parameter4::Float64 = 4.0,
    parameter5::String = "five", 
    parameter6::String = "six",
    parameter7::String = "seven",
    parameter8::String = "eight",
    save_to::String = nothing
) 
    # do some work 
    if !isnothing(save_to)
        # save the work
    end
end

experiment()
```

This code produces the following error:

```julia
MethodError: no method matching var"#experiment#159"(::Float64, ::Float64, ::Bool, ::Float64, ::String, ::String, ::String, ::String, ::Nothing, ::typeof(experiment))
The function `#experiment#159` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types.

Closest candidates are:
  var"#experiment#159"(::Float64, ::Float64, ::Bool, ::Float64, ::String, ::String, ::String, ::String, !Matched::String, ::typeof(experiment))
   @ Main ~/.julia/dev/GraphPPL.jl/jl_notebook_cell_df34fa98e69747e1a8f8a730347b8e2f_X10sZmlsZQ==.jl:1
```

- The function is identified as `#experiment#159`, which is an internal Julia detail and irrelevant for a regular user. This naming convention adds confusion without aiding in debugging.
- The error message highlights `!Matched::String` but does not specify which parameter is causing the issue. Given several String-typed parameters, this requires counting parameters to determine which one is problematic.
- The primary issue is with `save_to::String = nothing`, where the default value `nothing` doesn’t match the declared type `String`. However, the error message gives no indication of this mismatch or a clear way to identify it visually in a function with many parameters.

Some suggestions:

- The error message should specify the keyword argument name causing the type mismatch (e.g., `save_to`), allowing users to locate the issue more directly.
- Replace `#experiment#159` with a more user-friendly indication, like experiment, and omit the internal method name where possible.
- If the default value type conflicts with the declared parameter type, highlight this in the error message.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56554/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56554/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

{"action":"closed","number":56588,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56588","id":2184046123,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM6CLeYr","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56588","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56588.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56588.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56588","number":56588,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Buildkite Test Engine: fix JSON comma separate bug","user":{"login":"pda","id":15759,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE1NzU5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/15759?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda","html_url":"https://github.com/pda","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pda/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"This pull request fixes a missing-comma-separator bug in the [Bootleg JSON writer](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/7eb5cb89fb938a1dc67efa3861b25562767a7bbe/test/buildkitetestjson.jl#L13) 🏴‍☠️ which is responsible for producing JSON test results for [Buildkite Test Engine](https://buildkite.com/platform/test-engine/).

There was a bug when serializing to JSON array (from `AbstractVector`) where elements that were [`===` / “egal”](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/base/base/#Core.:===) to the final element would not have a comma separator placed after them, producing invalid JSON. I stumbled upon this because I work at Buildkite; I've previously [contributed a fix for a different issue in `test/buildkitetestjson.jl](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53706).

Specifically, this bug occurred when the last item of `expanded` or `backtrace` within `failure_expanded` was an empty string, because `elt === last(val)` was `"" === ""` was `true`, so e.g. `["hello", "", "world", ""]` would serialize to:

```json
[
  "hello",
  ""
  "world",
  ""
]
```

Note this was _not_ happening when the last element was a **non**-empty string; the strings come from `split(string, '\n')` which returns a singleton empty string for zero-width splits, but separate strings for non-empty splits:

```
julia> [[x, objectid(x)] for x in split("\n", '\n')]
2-element Vector{Vector{Any}}:
 ["", 0x37373e5ed3d3af9c]
 ["", 0x37373e5ed3d3af9c]

julia> [[x, objectid(x)] for x in split("hello\nhello", '\n')]
2-element Vector{Vector{Any}}:
 ["hello", 0x8096d78ab9fd726c]
 ["hello", 0x2bec5d6f24b3bdf5]
```

This patch changes the “is final item” check to be index-based, rather than identity-based.

I've tested it by adding a synthetic failure to an existing test:

```julia
@test throw(ErrorException("testing Buildkite JSON\n\ncomma after blank lines\n\nwhen last line is blank\n"))
```

And then observed the `test/results_1.json` before and after this patch:

```diff
       "expanded": [
         "testing Buildkite JSON",
-        ""
+        "",
         "comma after blank lines",
-        ""
+        "",
         "when last line is blank",
         ""
       ]
```

Related:
- https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53706
- cc: @tecosaur for past context

`eltype` method had an incorrect type declaration. The preexisting tests did not detect this because they check for array equality, where `Any[] == Char[]` is satisfied.

### Before

```julia-repl
julia> x = readeach(IOBuffer(), Char);

julia> eltype(x)
Any

julia> collect(x)
Any[]
```

### After

```julia-repl
julia> x = readeach(IOBuffer(), Char);

julia> eltype(x)
Char

julia> collect(x)
Char[]
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56678/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56678/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2499126109","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56678#issuecomment-2499126109","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56678","id":2499126109,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6U9aNd","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-25T22:01:49Z","updated_at":"2024-11-25T22:01:49Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Windows algebra","color":"f2b3db","default":false,"description":"Linear algebra"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2017-04-28T17:06:54Z","updated_at":"2024-11-26T11:28:08Z","closed_at":"2017-04-29T16:58:20Z","author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I find it surprising that this is the case for `Symmetric` (and `Hermitian`):
```julia
julia> typeof(Symmetric(Symmetric(rand(2, 2))))
Symmetric{Float64,Symmetric{Float64,Array{Float64,2}}}
```
instead of just `Symmetric{Float64,Array{Float64,2}}`. Is this behaviour ever useful?

The simplest solution would be to define these:

```julia
Symmetric(A::Symmetric, uplo::Symbol=:U) = A
Hermitian(A::Hermitian, uplo::Symbol=:U) = A
```
and just ignore the `uplo`. A more general fix would be the following, but that will sometimes create a new `Symmetric`/`Hermitian` instance, depending on the input `uplo`:

```julia
function Symmetric(A::Symmetric{T,S}, uplo::Symbol=:U) where {T,S}
    if A.uplo == char_uplo(uplo)
        return A
    else
        transpose!(A.data, copy(A.data)) # to keep correct aliasing
        return Symmetric{T,S}(A.data, char_uplo(uplo))
    end

Partial matching constrains the ways the CLI can be modified in the future. For example any prefix of a current option cannot safely be made a new option in case it takes over the behavior of a script that's using that partial match.

We have tab-completion and short options like `-p` so this kind of shortcut shouldn't be necessary. 

I suggest deprecating and removing partial matching.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56010/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":1,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56010/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

{"repository_id":430564540,"push_id":20574869000,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"9483de85a3e59f017330f6f36a083e7951262e87","before":"ad5e2b9c77515d4aab48a11c13df485328216bfd","commits":[{"sha":"9483de85a3e59f017330f6f36a083e7951262e87","author":{"email":"c9fed246bcfe477bbc1f7db2e4d8c6d92b09bd82@gmail.com","name":"Fredrik Ekre"},"message":"Remove `Base.convert` methods between `AbstractString` and `Kind` (#500)

* Remove the method `convert(::Type{String}, ::Kind)`

This patch removes the method `convert(::Type{String}, ::Kind)` used for
converting kinds to strings and replaces it with the already existing
method of `Base.string`. There are two reason for this: i) the method
causes invalidations when loading the package and ii) `convert` is
called implicitly in e.g. constructors and should therefore typically
only be defined between similar enough types.

* Remove the method `Base.convert(::Type{Kind}, ::String)`

This patch removes the method `Base.convert(::Type{Kind}, ::AbstractString)`
and replaces it with a `Kind(::AbstractString)` constructor. The reason
for this is that `convert` is called implicitly in e.g. constructors and
should therefore typically only be defined between similar enough types.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/commits/9483de85a3e59f017330f6f36a083e7951262e87"}]}

suggestion by @tecosaur, also use the heavy right box drawing character.  Can be tested with:

```julia
function test_progress(n, dt)
   io = stdout
   bar = Pkg.MiniProgressBars.MiniProgressBar(; indent=1, header = "Downloading packages", color = Base.info_color(),
                                    mode=:int, always_reprint=true)
   bar.max = n
   for i in 1:n
      bar.current = i
      Pkg.MiniProgressBars.show_progress(io, bar)
      sleep(dt)
   end
end
```

current behavior is:

```julia
julia> using LinearAlgebra

julia> norm((1, 2, 3))
3.7416573867739413

julia> cross((1, 2, 3), (4, 5, 6))
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching cross(::Tuple{Int64, Int64, Int64}, ::Tuple{Int64, Int64, Int64})
The function `cross` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types.

Closest candidates are:
  cross(::AbstractVector, ::AbstractVector)
```

with this PR:

```julia
julia> cross((1, 2, 3), (4, 5, 6))
(3, -6, 3)
```

The PR is pretty minimal, basically all I did was move the current code to the 3-tuple version, and then wrap it with a `collect` in the vector {"repository_id":430564540,"push_id":20066147973,"size":2,"distinct_size":2,"ref":"refs/heads/fe/convert","head":"58b31ef1c2875f8c11b64c3ca359a4065ca4ccbe","before":"2586be7a546b263d9b5ef98e1942edfb39029231","commits":[{"sha":"15abadd2c9394e7d0f9e04545301b04dc6037522","author":{"email":"c9fed246bcfe477bbc1f7db2e4d8c6d92b09bd82@gmail.com","name":"Fredrik Ekre"},"message":"Remove the method `convert(::Type{String}, ::Kind)`

This patch removes the method `convert(::Type{String}, ::Kind)` used for
converting kinds to strings and replaces it with the already existing
method of `Base.string`. There are two reason for this: i) the method
causes invalidations when loading the package and ii) `convert` is
called implicitly in e.g. constructors and should therefore typically
only be defined between similar enough types.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/commits/15abadd2c9394e7d0f9e04545301b04dc6037522"},{"sha":"58b31ef1c2875f8c11b64c3ca359a4065ca4ccbe","author":{"email":"c9fed246bcfe477bbc1f7db2e4d8c6d92b09bd82@gmail.com","name":"Fredrik Ekre"},"message":"Remove the method `Base.convert(::Type{Kind}, ::String)`

This patch removes the method `Base.convert(::Type{Kind}, ::AbstractString)`
and replaces it with a `Kind(::AbstractString)` constructor. The reason
for this is that `convert` is called implicitly in e.g. constructors and
should therefore typically only be defined between similar enough types.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/commits/58b31ef1c2875f8c11b64c3ca359a4065ca4ccbe"}]}

{"action":"closed","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55692","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55692/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55692/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55692/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55692","id":2506634184,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6VaDPI","number":55692,"title":"read(stdin, Char) behaves differently in REPL vs when run as a script in VSCode","user":{"login":"sadish-d","id":109114416,"node_id":"U_kgDOBoD0MA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/109114416?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d","html_url":"https://github.com/sadish-d","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sadish-d/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-05T02:26:01Z","updated_at":"2024-09-05T10:47:59Z","closed_at":"2024-09-05T10:47:59Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I have a .jl file with one line script:

```
i = read(stdin, Char)
```

If I run it in VSCode using the play button, and type `ab` in the REPL (without pressing the return key), I get:

```
a'b': ASCII/Unicode U+0062 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)

julia> i
'b': ASCII/Unicode U+0062 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)
```

`i` takes the second character entered: `b`.

Here is the behavior in REPL (need to press the return key after typing ab)

```
julia> i = read(stdin, Char)
ab
'a': ASCII/Unicode U+0061 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)

julia> b
ERROR: UndefVarError: `b` not defined

julia> i
'a': ASCII/Unicode U+0061 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)
```

`i` takes the first character entered: `a`.

possibly related: https://github.com/julia-vscode/julia-vscode/issues/785

versioninfo:
```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.10.3
Commit 0b4590a5507 (2024-04-30 10:59 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:
  OS: macOS (arm64-apple-darwin22.4.0)
  CPU: 8 × Apple M1
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-15.0.7 (ORCJIT, apple-m1)
Threads: 1 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 4 virtual cores)
Environment:

```
i = read(stdin, Char)
```

If I run it in VSCode using the play button, and type `ab` in the REPL (without pressing the return key), I get:

```
a'b': ASCII/Unicode U+0062 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)

julia> i
'b': ASCII/Unicode U+0062 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)
```

`i` takes the second character entered: `b`.

Here is the behavior in REPL (need to press the return key after typing ab)

```
julia> i = read(stdin, Char)
ab
'a': ASCII/Unicode U+0061 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)

julia> b
ERROR: UndefVarError: `b` not defined

julia> i
'a': ASCII/Unicode U+0061 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)
```

`i` takes the first character entered: `a`.

possibly related: https://github.com/julia-vscode/julia-vscode/issues/785

{"action":"closed","number":55739,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55739","id":2066696605,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM57L0md","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55739","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55739.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55739.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55739","number":55739,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Add `invokelatest` barrier to `string(...)` in `@assert`","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This change protects `@assert` from invalidations to `Base.string(...)` by adding an `invokelatest` barrier.

A common source of invalidations right now is `print(io, join(args...))`. The problem is:
1. Inference concludes that `join(::Any...)` returns `Union{String,AnnotatedString}`
2. The `print` call is union-split to `String` and `AnnotatedString`
3. This code is now invalidated when StyledStrings defines `print(io, ::AnnotatedString)`

The invalidation chain for `@assert` is similar: ` @assert 1 == 1` calls into `string(::Expr)` which calls into `print(io, join(args::Any...))`. Unfortunately that leads to the invalidation of almost all `@assert`s without an explicit error message

Similar to {"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":20200936097,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"945517ba4e15f7470b8790a696ba5404ef047f2f","before":"22eded8bbfeb6557c012e4b78c3c69c993d0d4e9","commits":[{"sha":"945517ba4e15f7470b8790a696ba5404ef047f2f","author":{"email":"a87f6485e8aa8163af7a61acec9c8b75e685fcec@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Cody Tapscott"},"message":"Add `invokelatest` barrier to `string(...)` in `@assert` (#55739)

This change protects `@assert` from invalidations to `Base.string(...)`
by adding an `invokelatest` barrier.

A common source of invalidations right now is `print(io,
join(args...))`. The problem is:
1. Inference concludes that `join(::Any...)` returns
`Union{String,AnnotatedString}`
2. The `print` call is union-split to `String` and `AnnotatedString`
3. This code is now invalidated when StyledStrings defines `print(io,
::AnnotatedString)`

The invalidation chain for `@assert` is similar: ` @assert 1 == 1` calls
into `string(::Expr)` which calls into `print(io, join(args::Any...))`.
Unfortunately that leads to the invalidation of almost all `@assert`s
without an explicit error message

Similar to
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55583#issuecomment-2308969806","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/945517ba4e15f7470b8790a696ba5404ef047f2f"}]}

code"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":22,"created_at":"2024-09-13T20:19:29Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T22:26:05Z","closed_at":"2024-09-24T22:11:43Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55767","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55767","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55767.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55767.patch","merged_at":"2024-09-24T22:11:43Z"},"body":"In order to help avoid memory provenance issues, and better utilize stack space (somewhat), and use FCA less, change the preferred representation of an immutable object to be a pair of `<packed-data,roots>` values. This packing requires some care at the boundaries and if the expected field alignment exceeds that of a pointer. The change is expected to eventually make codegen more flexible at representing unions of values with both bits and pointer regions.

Eventually we can also have someone improve the late-gc-lowering pass to take advantage of this increased information accuracy, but currently it will not be any better than before at laying out the frame.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55767/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55767/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2375375538","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55767#issuecomment-2375375538","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55767","id":2375375538,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6NlVqy","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-25T22:26:04Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T22:26:04Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"This seems to have broken SIMD.jl

still needs tests, but I'm putting it up as a PR first to get feedback. The intended goal here is to make the `dolinsolve` functionality in OrdinaryDiffEq of being able to pass in a `precs` function that takes in `A` and possibly some parameters and remakes your preconditioner for the new `A` value.

Here's an example of the new functionality in action:
```
using LinearSolve, Krylov, KrylovPreconditioners, LinearAlgebra, SparseArrays
A, b = sprand(10,10, .5), rand(10);
prob = LinearProblem(A,b)
solver = KrylovJL_CG(precs=(A,p=nothing) -> (BlockJacobiPreconditioner(A, 2), I), ldiv=false)
cache = init(prob, solver)
solve!(cache)
reinit!(cache, A = sprand(10,10, .2), b = rand(10))
solve!(cache)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/514/reactions","total_count":3,"+1":3,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/514/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/comments/2275662004","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/pull/514#issuecomment-2275662004","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/514","id":2275662004,"node_id":"IC_kwDOFsu4xM6Ho9i0","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-08T12:08:23Z","updated_at":"2024-08-08T12:08:23Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"https://github.com/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/pull/501#issuecomment-2109009251  map(f, it, iters...) = collect(Generator(f, it, iters...))
 
+# Generic versions of push! for AbstractVector
+# These are specialized further for Vector for faster resizing and setindexing
+function push!(a::AbstractVector{T}, item) where T
+    # convert first so we don't grow the array if the assignment won't work
+    itemT = item isa T ? item : convert(T, item)::T
+    new_length = length(a) + 1
+    resize!(a, new_length)
+    a[new_length] = itemT
+    return a
+end
+
+# specialize and optimize the single argument case
+function push!(a::AbstractVector{Any}, @nospecialize x)
+    new_length = length(a) + 1
+    resize!(a, new_length)
+    a[new_length] = x
+    return a
+end
+function push!(a::AbstractVector{Any}, @nospecialize x...)
+    @_terminates_locally_meta
+    na = length(a)
+    nx = length(x)
+    resize!(a, na + nx)
+    for i = 1:nx
+        a[na+i] = x[i]
+    end
+    return a
+end
+

 push!(A, a, b, c...) = push!(push!(A, a, b), c...)
 pushfirst!(A, a, b) = pushfirst!(pushfirst!(A, b), a)
 pushfirst!(A, a, b, c...) = pushfirst!(pushfirst!(A, c...), a, b)

In Julia 1.10, `push!` and `append!` would be functional for `AbstractVector` implementations
if `resize!` and `setindex!` were defined.

As of #51903 by @vtjnash as in Julia 1.11.0-rc2, `append!` now depends on an implementation of
`sizehint!` and `push!`. Since `push!` also depends on `append!`, a stack
overflow situation can easily be created.

To avoid this, this pull request defines the following

* Add generic versions of `push!(a::AbstractVector, x)` which do not depend on `append!`
* Add default implementation of `sizehint!` that is a no-op

The implementation of `push!(a::AbstractVector, x)` is a generic version based on the implementation
of `push!(a::Vector, x)` without depending on internals.

# Example for SimpleArray

Consider the `SimpleArray` example from test/abstractarray.jl:

```julia
mutable struct SimpleArray{T} <: AbstractVector{T}
    els::Vector{T}
end
Base.size(sa::SimpleArray) = size(sa.els)
Base.getindex(sa::SimpleArray, idx...) = getindex(sa.els, idx...)
Base.setindex!(sa::SimpleArray, v, idx...) = setindex!(sa.els, v, idx...)
Base.resize!(sa::SimpleArray, n) = resize!(sa.els, n)
Base.copy(sa::SimpleArray) = SimpleArray(copy(sa.els))
```

Note that `setindex!` and `resize!` are implemented for `SimpleArray`.

## Julia 1.10.4: push! is functional

On Julia 1.10.4, `push!` has a functional implementation for `SimpleArray`

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int,5)), 6)
6-element SimpleArray{Int64}:
 0
 0
 0
 0
 0
 6
```

## Julia 1.11.0-rc2 and nightly: push! requires sizehint! and is prone to stack overflow

Before this pull request, on Julia 1.11.0-rc2 and nightly, `push!` fails for want of `sizehint!`.

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int,5)), 6)
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching sizehint!(::SimpleArray{Int64}, ::Int64)
The function `sizehint!` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types.
...
```

After implementing `sizehint!`, `push!` still fails with a stack overflow.

```julia-repl
julia> Base.sizehint!(a::SimpleArray, x) = a

julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int, 5)), 6)
Warning: detected a stack overflow; program state may be corrupted, so further execution might be unreliable.
ERROR: StackOverflowError:
Stacktrace:
      [1] _append!
        @ ./array.jl:1344 [inlined]
      [2] append!
        @ ./array.jl:1335 [inlined]
      [3] push!(a::SimpleArray{Int64}, iter::Int64)
        @ Base ./array.jl:1336
--- the above 3 lines are repeated 79982 more times ---
 [239950] _append!
        @ ./array.jl:1344 [inlined]
 [239951] append!
        @ ./array.jl:1335 [inlined]
```

This is because the new implementation of `append!` depends on `push!`.

## After this pull request, push! is functional.

After this pull request, there is a functional `push!` for `SimpleArray` again as in Julia 1.10.4:

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int, 5), 6)
6-element SimpleArray{Int64}:
 0
 0
 0
 0
 0
 6
```

 """
 map(f, it, iters...) = collect(Generator(f, it, iters...))
 
+# Generic versions of push! for AbstractVector
+# These are specialized further for Vector for faster resizing and setindexing
+function push!(a::AbstractVector{T}, item) where T
+    # convert first so we don't grow the array if the assignment won't work
+    itemT = item isa T ? item : convert(T, item)::T
+    new_length = length(a) + 1
+    resize!(a, new_length)

In Julia 1.10, `push!` and `append!` would be functional for `AbstractVector` implementations
if `resize!` and `setindex!` were defined.

As of #51903 by @vtjnash as in Julia 1.11.0-rc2, `append!` now depends on an implementation of
`sizehint!` and `push!`. Since `push!` also depends on `append!`, a stack
overflow situation can easily be created.

To avoid this, this pull request defines the following

* Add generic versions of `push!(a::AbstractVector, x)` which do not depend on `append!`
* Add default implementation of `sizehint!` that is a no-op

The implementation of `push!(a::AbstractVector, x)` is a generic version based on the implementation
of `push!(a::Vector, x)` without depending on internals.

# Example for SimpleArray

Consider the `SimpleArray` example from test/abstractarray.jl:

```julia
mutable struct SimpleArray{T} <: AbstractVector{T}
    els::Vector{T}
end
Base.size(sa::SimpleArray) = size(sa.els)
Base.getindex(sa::SimpleArray, idx...) = getindex(sa.els, idx...)
Base.setindex!(sa::SimpleArray, v, idx...) = setindex!(sa.els, v, idx...)
Base.resize!(sa::SimpleArray, n) = resize!(sa.els, n)
Base.copy(sa::SimpleArray) = SimpleArray(copy(sa.els))
```

Note that `setindex!` and `resize!` are implemented for `SimpleArray`.

## Julia 1.10.4: push! is functional

On Julia 1.10.4, `push!` has a functional implementation for `SimpleArray`

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int,5)), 6)
6-element SimpleArray{Int64}:
 0
 0
 0
 0
 0
 6
```

## Julia 1.11.0-rc2 and nightly: push! requires sizehint! and is prone to stack overflow

Before this pull request, on Julia 1.11.0-rc2 and nightly, `push!` fails for want of `sizehint!`.

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int,5)), 6)
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching sizehint!(::SimpleArray{Int64}, ::Int64)
The function `sizehint!` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types.
...
```

After implementing `sizehint!`, `push!` still fails with a stack overflow.

```julia-repl
julia> Base.sizehint!(a::SimpleArray, x) = a

julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int, 5)), 6)
Warning: detected a stack overflow; program state may be corrupted, so further execution might be unreliable.
ERROR: StackOverflowError:
Stacktrace:
      [1] _append!
        @ ./array.jl:1344 [inlined]
      [2] append!
        @ ./array.jl:1335 [inlined]
      [3] push!(a::SimpleArray{Int64}, iter::Int64)
        @ Base ./array.jl:1336
--- the above 3 lines are repeated 79982 more times ---
 [239950] _append!
        @ ./array.jl:1344 [inlined]
 [239951] append!
        @ ./array.jl:1335 [inlined]
```

This is because the new implementation of `append!` depends on `push!`.

## After this pull request, push! is functional.

After this pull request, there is a functional `push!` for `SimpleArray` again as in Julia 1.10.4:

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int, 5), 6)
6-element SimpleArray{Int64}:
 0
 0
 0
 0
 0
 6
```

implementation for AbstractArray depending only on resize!","user":{"login":"mkitti","id":8062771,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgwNjI3NzE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8062771?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti","html_url":"https://github.com/mkitti","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mkitti/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Fix #55459

In Julia 1.10, `push!` and `append!` would be functional for `AbstractVector` implementations
if `resize!` and `setindex!` were defined.

As of #51903 by @vtjnash as in Julia 1.11.0-rc2, `append!` now depends on an implementation of
`sizehint!` and `push!`. Since `push!` also depends on `append!`, a stack
overflow situation can easily be created.

To avoid this, this pull request defines the following

* Add generic versions of `push!(a::AbstractVector, x)` which do not depend on `append!`
* Add default implementation of `sizehint!` that is a no-op

The implementation of `push!(a::AbstractVector, x)` is a generic version based on the implementation
of `push!(a::Vector, x)` without depending on internals.

# Example for SimpleArray

Consider the `SimpleArray` example from test/abstractarray.jl:

```julia
mutable struct SimpleArray{T} <: AbstractVector{T}
    els::Vector{T}
end
Base.size(sa::SimpleArray) = size(sa.els)
Base.getindex(sa::SimpleArray, idx...) = getindex(sa.els, idx...)
Base.setindex!(sa::SimpleArray, v, idx...) = setindex!(sa.els, v, idx...)
Base.resize!(sa::SimpleArray, n) = resize!(sa.els, n)
Base.copy(sa::SimpleArray) = SimpleArray(copy(sa.els))
```

Note that `setindex!` and `resize!` are implemented for `SimpleArray`.

## Julia 1.10.4: push! is functional

On Julia 1.10.4, `push!` has a functional implementation for `SimpleArray`

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int,5)), 6)
6-element SimpleArray{Int64}:
 0
 0
 0
 0
 0
 6
```

## Julia 1.11.0-rc2 and nightly: push! requires sizehint! and is prone to stack overflow

Before this pull request, on Julia 1.11.0-rc2 and nightly, `push!` fails for want of `sizehint!`.

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int,5)), 6)
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching sizehint!(::SimpleArray{Int64}, ::Int64)
The function `sizehint!` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types.
...
```

After implementing `sizehint!`, `push!` still fails with a stack overflow.

```julia-repl
julia> Base.sizehint!(a::SimpleArray, x) = a

julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int, 5)), 6)
Warning: detected a stack overflow; program state may be corrupted, so further execution might be unreliable.
ERROR: StackOverflowError:
Stacktrace:
      [1] _append!
        @ ./array.jl:1344 [inlined]
      [2] append!
        @ ./array.jl:1335 [inlined]
      [3] push!(a::SimpleArray{Int64}, iter::Int64)
        @ Base ./array.jl:1336
--- the above 3 lines are repeated 79982 more times ---
 [239950] _append!
        @ ./array.jl:1344 [inlined]
 [239951] append!
        @ ./array.jl:1335 [inlined]
```

This is because the new implementation of `append!` depends on `push!`.

## After this pull request, push! is functional.

After this pull request, there is a functional `push!` for `SimpleArray` again as in Julia 1.10.4:

```julia-repl
julia> push!(SimpleArray{Int}(zeros(Int, 5), 6)
6-element SimpleArray{Int64}:
 0
 0
 0
 0
 0
 6
```

+
+"""
+    uripath(path::AbstractString)
+
+Encode `path` as a URI as per [RFC8089: The "file" URI
+Scheme](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8089), [RFC3986: Uniform Resource
+Identifier (URI): Generic Syntax](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986), and
+the [Freedesktop File URI spec](https://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Specifications/file-uri-spec/).
+
+## Examples
+
+```julia-repl
+julia> uripath("/home/user/example file.jl") # On a unix machine
+"file://<hostname>/home/user/example%20file.jl"
+
+juila> uripath("C:\\Users\\user\\example file.jl") # On a windows machine
+"file:///C:/Users/user/example%20file.jl"
+```
+"""
+function uripath end
+
+@static if Sys.iswindows()
+    function uripath(path::String)
+        percent_escape(s) = # RFC3986 Section 2.1
+            '%' * join(map(b -> uppercase(string(b, base=16)), codeunits(s)), '%')
+        encode_uri_component(s) = # RFC3986 Section 2.3
+            replace(s, r"[^A-Za-z0-9\-_.~/]+" => percent_escape)
+        path = abspath(path)
+        if startswith(path, "\\\\") # UNC path, RFC8089 Appendix E.3
+            unixpath = join(eachsplit(path, path_separator_re, keepempty=false), '/')
+            string("file://", encode_uri_component(unixpath)) # RFC8089 Section 2
+        else
+            drive, localpath = splitdrive(path) # Assuming that non-UNC absolute paths on Windows always have a drive component
+            unixpath = join(eachsplit(localpath, path_separator_re, keepempty=false), '/')
+            encdrive = replace(encode_uri_component(drive), "%3A" => ':', "%7C" => '|') # RFC8089 Appendices D.2, E.2.1, and E.2.2
+            string("file:///", encdrive, '/', encode_uri_component(unixpath)) # RFC8089 Section 2
+        end
+    end
+else

+        percent_escape(s) = # RFC3986 Section 2.1

# Minimum Reproducible Example

1. Create a file named `asd.jl` with the following content:

```julia
mutable struct Range
	start::UInt32
	finish::UInt32
end

function complement_range(input::Range, exclude::Range)::Vector{Range}
	not_overlapping::Bool = input.start > exclude.finish || input.finish < exclude.start
	if not_overlapping
		return [Range(input.start, input.finish)]
	end

	result::Vector{Range} = []
	if input.start < exclude.start
		append!(result, [Range(input.start, exclude.start)])
	end
	if input.finish > exclude.finish
		append!(result, [Range(exclude.finish, input.finish)])
	end
	return result
end

function test_complement_range()::Nothing
	result = complement_range(Range(1, 10), Range(3, 7))

	println("result::", typeof(result), ": ", result)
	println("[Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]::",
		typeof([Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]), ": ",
		"[Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]"
	)
	println(
		"complement_range(Range(1, 10), Range(3, 7)) == [Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]: ",
		complement_range(Range(1, 10), Range(3, 7)) == [Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]
	)

	@assert [Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)] == result

	result = complement_range(Range(10, 20), Range(3, 7))
	@assert [Range(10, 20)] == result

	result = complement_range(Range(1, 3), Range(3, 7))
	@assert [Range(1, 3)] == result

	result = complement_range(Range(7, 10), Range(3, 7))
	@assert [Range(7, 10)] == result
end

test_complement_range()
```

2. Run `include("asd.jl")` to run the script in the Julia REPL
- or by executing `cmd` in the file path bar of Windows Explorer and run the file with `julia asd.jl`.

# Expected Output

The expected output should be:

```jl
result::Vector{Range}: [Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]
[Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]::Vector{Range}: [Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]
complement_range(Range(1, 10), Range(3, 7)) == [Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]: true
```

# Actual Output

[Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]::Vector{Range}: [Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]
complement_range(Range(1, 10), Range(3, 7)) == [Range(1, 3), Range(7, 10)]: false

{"action":"closed","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55054","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55054/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55054/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55054/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55054","id":2393676073,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6OrJkp","number":55054,"title":"Multi-editor syntax highlighting issue with `using ...: func! as func` ","user":{"login":"MilesCranmer","id":7593028,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc1OTMwMjg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7593028?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer","html_url":"https://github.com/MilesCranmer","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MilesCranmer/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-07-06T19:13:01Z","updated_at":"2024-07-07T14:29:42Z","closed_at":"2024-07-07T14:29:42Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"```julia
using Library: my_func! as my_func
```

As you can see, `as` is not colored. I see this in:
- GitHub Linguist (https://github.com/github-linguist/linguist), which is shown above, and
- VSCode

I do _not_ see this issue in:

- Vim

However, removing the exclamation mark fixes this on all of these platforms:

```julia
using Library: my_func as my_func
```

Although the Sublime syntax highlighter (https://github.com/JuliaEditorSupport/Julia-sublime/tree/3366b10be91aaab7a61ae0bc0a5af5cc375e58d1) which is used in other packages like [`bat`](https://github.com/sharkdp/bat), seems to skip highlighting the `as` entirely.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55054/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55054/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

{"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":19292898583,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"aba67661f91fa954c8f40dce1ac2d8d6bacdaee3","before":"2a8bdd0812f621b38fd11411c99f357b4bc0b402","commits":[{"sha":"aba67661f91fa954c8f40dce1ac2d8d6bacdaee3","author":{"email":"c9fed246bcfe477bbc1f7db2e4d8c6d92b09bd82@gmail.com","name":"Fredrik Ekre"},"message":"Color todo admonitions in the REPL (#54957)

This patch adds magenta coloring for `!!! todo` admonitions in addition
to the existing styling of `danger`, `warning`, `info`, `note`, `tip`,
and `compat` admonitions. This is useful if you want to leave some more
colorful todo notes in docstrings, for example.

Accompanying PR for Documenter to render these in HTML and PDF docs:

requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-07-15T18:25:57Z","updated_at":"2024-07-15T19:47:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55131","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55131","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55131.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55131.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Implement `@save` debugging tool similar to `@show`, but instead of printing, saves the captured values to globals in Main.

````
    @save variable
    @save (variable | variable=expression)...

Save `variable` for manual user inspection.

When using the default [`ConsoleLogger`](@ref), Sets `Main.varaible` equal to the result
of `expression`, or the value of `variable` if `expression` is not provided.

Behavior may be overridden by a custom logging backend. See [`@logmsg`](@ref) for more info.

# Examples

```jldoctest
julia> function f(x)
           x -= 1
           @save x
           1/x
       end
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> f(1)
Inf

julia> x
0
```
````

---

Passes through Logging so that results can be aggregated or redirected by custom logging backends. e.g. you might want to save all the results of an `@save` callsite instead of only the most recent.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55131/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55131/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2229259090","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55131#issuecomment-2229259090","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55131","id":2229259090,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6E38tS","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-07-15T19:47:12Z","updated_at":"2024-07-15T19:47:12Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"This looks like https://juliadebug.github.io/Infiltrator.jl/stable/API/#Infiltrator.@exfiltrate. This was a bit of a surprise to me actually. Since there are both Whitespace and Newline(Ws) tokens it seems a bit strange to have them combined. On the other hand, for the purpose of writing a code formatter it was rather convenient: Stripping trailing space is easy since I only need to find NewlineWs nodes, and indents is also easy for the same reason (no need to peek backwards and forward).


Also, can we have a new release including this and #455 ? Is there a reason releases aren't cut from the main branch? requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":7,"created_at":"2024-07-15T18:25:57Z","updated_at":"2024-07-19T10:03:53Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55131","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55131","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55131.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55131.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Implement `@save` debugging tool similar to `@show`, but instead of printing, saves the captured values to globals in Main.

````
    @save variable
    @save (variable | variable=expression)...

Save `variable` for manual user inspection.

When using the default [`ConsoleLogger`](@ref), Sets `Main.varaible` equal to the result
of `expression`, or the value of `variable` if `expression` is not provided.

Behavior may be overridden by a custom logging backend. See [`@logmsg`](@ref) for more info.

# Examples

```jldoctest
julia> function f(x)
           x -= 1
           @save x
           1/x
       end
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> f(1)
Inf

julia> x
0
```
````

---

Passes through Logging so that results can be aggregated or redirected by custom logging backends. e.g. you might want to save all the results of an `@save` callsite instead of only the most What I've found to be super useful in the past for this kind of debugging is to emit csv lines with these fields: timestamp, node id, process id, variable name, value.

These thing is pretty easy to achieve already with LoggingExtras + e.g. the [JSON logging format](https://github.com/JuliaLogging/LoggingFormats.jl?tab=readme-ov-file#json-output-log-events-as-json).

The `handle_message` changes in this PR really seems like the kind of specialization you do for a new logger type and dispatch on it, `SaveLogger` or something. {"repository_id":430564540,"push_id":19409593933,"size":10,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/release-0.4","head":"7e444ae0eb6b918aab4e11428a35db0d05c2d5e9","before":"b1c758cb7e770e13b35cff7940464449cd8f6b79","commits":[{"sha":"db9a9c48be82372c852f75f95092a54833776e7d","author":{"email":"c9fed246bcfe477bbc1f7db2e4d8c6d92b09bd82@gmail.com","name":"Fredrik Ekre"},"message":"Fix tokenization of emitting comments followed by `\r\n` (#455)

Before this patch the `\r` character would be part of the comment and
not the following NewlineWs (which would simply be a `\n`-style
NewlineWs instead of a `\r\n` as one would expect).

(cherry picked from commit 1d950817b8ab62a3be2464f4d5b097aa68c17042, PR #455)","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/commits/db9a9c48be82372c852f75f95092a54833776e7d"},{"sha":"ef894e20ee4dead8625749c59a6ece74467abb84","author":{"email":"c9fed246bcfe477bbc1f7db2e4d8c6d92b09bd82@gmail.com","name":"Fredrik Ekre"},"message":"Fix tokenization of consecutive `\r\n` line endings (#460)

Without this patch `\r\n\r\n` would be tokenized as `\r\n\r` and `\n`
instead of `\r\n` and `\r\n`. Fixes
https://github.com/fredrikekre/Runic.jl/issues/15.

(cherry picked from commit a11e664faea8ff394a5eeb82be651ceba03ee696, PR #460)","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/commits/ef894e20ee4dead8625749c59a6ece74467abb84"},{"sha":"34e9dbdc2a9e588168e72dd21ad4c9e84a952e5f","author":{"email":"c9fed246bcfe477bbc1f7db2e4d8c6d92b09bd82@gmail.com","name":"Fredrik Ekre"},"message":"Set version to 0.4.9.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/commits/34e9dbdc2a9e588168e72dd21ad4c9e84a952e5f"},{"sha":"2ecad8692038ce8242d1d6c47bbb90af98512caa","author":{"email":"f0988ce1004ccf6627c3386b41d8af367031c926@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Nathan Zimmerberg"},"message":"Update `Base.peek` docstring

they are essentially just identifiers which will be looked up with the usual scoping rules, just like any other identifier.

Code which processes syntax trees often has to do things like `is_operator(x) || kind(x) == K"Identifier"`. Mapping them to identifiers simplifies things and fixes this issue. For the same reasons, I've also wished for this in JuliaLowering.jl.

One caveat is that this change might be unhelpful for the `JuliaSyntax.tokenize()` API - need to think about what to do there.  (Perhaps operator kinds should be preserved in that case?)

If we do this, we should also do it systematically. For example, to fix https://github.com/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/pull/456/files#r1692517031","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/issues/474/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/issues/474/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/issues/comments/2252496006","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/issues/474#issuecomment-2252496006","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/JuliaSyntax.jl/issues/474","id":2252496006,"node_id":"IC_kwDOGankvM6GQlyG","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-07-26T10:47:28Z","updated_at":"2024-07-26T10:47:28Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"> Code which processes syntax trees often has to do things like is_operator(x) || kind(x) == K"Identifier".

call"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":107,"created_at":"2021-09-01T17:00:06Z","updated_at":"2024-06-04T15:16:39Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/42080","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/42080","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/42080.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/42080.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"I feel we are heading up against a "`using` crisis" where any new feature that is implemented by exporting a new name (either in Base or a package) becomes a breaking change. This is already happening (https://github.com/JuliaGPU/CUDA.jl/pull/1097, https://github.com/JuliaWeb/HTTP.jl/pull/745) and as projects get bigger and more names are exported, the likelihood of this rapidly increases.

The flaw in `using Foo` is fundamental in that you cannot lexically see where a name comes from so when two packages export the same name, you are screwed. Any code that relies on `using Foo` and then using an exported name from `Foo` is vulnerable to another dependency exporting the same name.
Therefore, I think we should start to strongly discourage the use of `using Foo` and only recommend `using Foo` for ephemeral work (e.g. REPL work).

feel we are heading up against a "`using` crisis" where any new feature that is implemented by exporting a new name (either in Base or a package) becomes a breaking change. This is already happening (https://github.com/JuliaGPU/CUDA.jl/pull/1097, https://github.com/JuliaWeb/HTTP.jl/pull/745) and as projects get bigger and more names are exported, the likelihood of this rapidly increases.

The flaw in `using Foo` is fundamental in that you cannot lexically see where a name comes from so when two packages export the same name, you are screwed. Any code that relies on `using Foo` and then using an exported name from `Foo` is vulnerable to another dependency exporting the same name.
Therefore, I think we should start to strongly discourage the use of `using Foo` and only recommend `using Foo` for ephemeral work (e.g. REPL work).

s]"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-05-29T17:58:58Z","updated_at":"2024-06-05T08:22:48Z","closed_at":"2024-06-05T08:22:48Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi everyone,

I hit some very weird corner case where I create views on vectors where the index set is a vector. This causes some troubles for alias analysis. A minimal example which should be efficient to perform due to the shared index set is to copy the values from one subvector to another one. 

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

indices = [1,3]
a = rand(3)
av = @view a[indices]
b = rand(3)
bv = @view b[indices]
@btime copyto!($av, $bv) # 2 allocations

av = @view a[[1,3]]
bv = @view b[[1,3]]
@btime copyto!($av, $bv) # 0 allocations
```

I discovered this on Julia 1.10, but from going down the call graph I also assume that it  is reproducible with older versions too, because it can be tracked down to mightalias returning true for the first case.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54621/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54621/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

Preserve top level dependencies better","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-05-03T08:57:38Z","updated_at":"2024-05-03T08:57:38Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"When adding package that is already in the manifest because of a top level dependency of a different project (or as a transient dependency, but those almost always track the registry) we should try to preserve the version. For example:
```
$ tree .
.
├── Manifest.toml
├── Project.toml
└── test
    └── Project.toml

$ cat Project.toml
[deps]
Example = "7876af07-990d-54b4-ab0e-23690620f79a"

[workspace]
projects = ["test"]

$ julia -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.status()'
Status `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/Project.toml`
  [7876af07] Example v0.5.4 `https://github.com/JuliaLang/Example.jl.git#master`

$ julia --project=test -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.status()'
Status `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/test/Project.toml` (empty project)

$ julia --project=test -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.add("Example")'
   Resolving package versions...
    Updating `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/test/Project.toml`
  [7876af07] + Example v0.5.3
    Updating `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/Manifest.toml`
  [7876af07] + Example v0.5.3

$ julia -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.status()'
Status `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/Project.toml`
  [7876af07] Example v0.5.3
```

I think this would be fixed by pinning top level dependencies of all projects in the first stage of the tiered resolver.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3890/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3890/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891","id":2277237531,"node_id":"I_kwDOBOhtSc6Hu-Mb","number":3891,"title":"[workspace] Misleading printing of Manifest.toml change","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-05-03T09:00:31Z","updated_at":"2024-05-03T09:00:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Example:
```
$ tree .
.
├── Manifest.toml
├── Project.toml
└── test
    └── Project.toml

$ cat Project.toml
[deps]
Example = "7876af07-990d-54b4-ab0e-23690620f79a"

[workspace]
projects = ["test"]

$ julia -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.status()'
Status `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/Project.toml`
  [7876af07] Example v0.5.4 `https://github.com/JuliaLang/Example.jl.git#master`

$ julia --project=test -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.status()'
Status `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/test/Project.toml` (empty project)

$ julia --project=test -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.add("Example")'
   Resolving package versions...
    Updating `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/test/Project.toml`
  [7876af07] + Example v0.5.3
    Updating `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/Manifest.toml`
  [7876af07] + Example v0.5.3
```

The last line there should be
```
  [7876af07] ~ Example v0.5.4 `https://github.com/JuliaLang/Example.jl.git#master` ⇒ v0.5.3
```
instead of
```
  [7876af07] + Example v0.5.3
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

have a minor issue when trying to `dev` a relative directory in REPL package mode.

My package has uppercase letters, say `MyPackage`, but I have moved there using the shell environment, having used a lower case letter by mistake:
```
shell> cd mypackage/test
```
which works.

`pwd()` gives the global path with `mypackage` in lower case letters, so the mistake is not "corrected".

If I activate the environment there (still works) and try to `dev` the parent folder, I get
```
(test) pkg> dev ..
ERROR: `..` appears to be a local path, but directory does not exist
```

Funnily, `FileSystem` does not have any issues:
```
julia> Base.Filesystem.readdir("..")
```
works as expected.

Also, this is not a `Pkg.jl` bug since
```
julia> import Pkg; Pkg.develop(path="..")
```
works as well.

Environment:

Misleading printing of Manifest.toml change","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6903156476,"node_id":"LA_kwDOBOhtSc8AAAABm3XO_A","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/labels/workspace","name":"workspace","color":"63C72A","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-05-03T09:00:31Z","updated_at":"2024-05-03T15:31:46Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Example:
```
$ tree .
.
├── Manifest.toml
├── Project.toml
└── test
    └── Project.toml

$ cat Project.toml
[deps]
Example = "7876af07-990d-54b4-ab0e-23690620f79a"

[workspace]
projects = ["test"]

$ julia -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.status()'
Status `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/Project.toml`
  [7876af07] Example v0.5.4 `https://github.com/JuliaLang/Example.jl.git#master`

$ julia --project=test -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.status()'
Status `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/test/Project.toml` (empty project)

$ julia --project=test -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.add("Example")'
   Resolving package versions...
    Updating `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/test/Project.toml`
  [7876af07] + Example v0.5.3
    Updating `/tmp/tmp.C9bJWtPVPR/Manifest.toml`
  [7876af07] + Example v0.5.3
```

The last line there should be
```
  [7876af07] ~ Example v0.5.4 `https://github.com/JuliaLang/Example.jl.git#master` ⇒ v0.5.3
```
instead of
```
  [7876af07] + Example v0.5.3
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/2093250172","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891#issuecomment-2093250172","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3891","id":2093250172,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBOhtSc58xHZ8","user":{"login":"fredrikekre","id":11698744,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjk4NzQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11698744?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre","html_url":"https://github.com/fredrikekre","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredrikekre/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-03T15:31:46Z","updated_at":"2024-05-03T15:31:46Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Okay, "Move profiling manual section to a new tutorials toplevel heading (#52056)"","user":{"login":"KristofferC","id":1282691,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyODI2OTE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1282691?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC","html_url":"https://github.com/KristofferC","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This reverts commit 137783f1663ae0f7c1129c7d8031c874083b49fe.

I think there are many problems with the new Tutorial toplevel section:

- It should be very important since it adds a full new toplevel section (to the existing five). However, it basically does not contain anything. It was created by a somewhat arbitrary move of a manual-entry to the tutorial section as well as a single page that more or less only contains an external link.

- There is a non-obvious overlap between the Manual entries and the Tutorial entries. The profiling entry was moved to tutorials but you could take more or less any other entry in the manual entry and move it as well.

- The comment in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52056#pullrequestreview-1721618599 says 
  > We need a proper place for tutorials so tutorial-like PRs don't get rejected and so that people can write actual tutorials content with the correct audience in mind

   While this might be true I don't think it is obvious at all that the manual needs to be the place where people can "dump" general tutorials about things. Right now, the Tutorial-section has more or less "unbounded scope" and it isn't clear at all what should go in there.

- It has been there for 7 months without any real activity (except taking a (again somewhat arbitrary) piece of the Pkg documentation into it) so it seems it isn't really used for anything.

  
Considering all of the above, I think it is best to go back to the status quo of 1.10 instead of "locking this in" for 1.11.
